name: Same Threat Detected on Multiple Servers (by Machine Identifier)
notes: This rule triggers when the same threat has been detected on multiple hosts that are servers. This could indicate the presence of a malware spreading in the network.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Endpoint Protection Devices, BB:DeviceDefinition: IDS / IPS
when an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Servers
when at least 5 events are seen with the same Threat Name (custom) and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
thresholds: when at least 5 events are seen with the same Threat Name (custom) and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Same Threat Detected on Multiple Servers (by Machine Identifier) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Endpoint Protection Devices, BB:DeviceDefinition: IDS / IPS
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Servers
AND when at least 5 events are seen with the same Threat Name (custom) and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Same Threat Detected on Multiple Servers (by Machine Identifier)
Event description: Same threat has been detected on multiple hosts that are servers.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Threat Name (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
